stories.title: Oops! People Are Betting on Singapore Pools From Overseas
stories.headline_kid: A news team discovered that people can use the Singapore Pools app to bet even when they are not in Singapore — even though the rules say they should not be able to!
summary: Singapore Pools is a company run by the government that lets people place bets on things like the lottery and football matches. But there is an important rule: you are only supposed to use the app while you are physically inside Singapore. This rule exists to help stop people from betting too much or losing control of their money, especially when they travel.

Reporters from CNA decided to test this rule. They brought the app with them to the United Kingdom and Italy, and to their surprise, they were able to place bets successfully in both countries! Other users say the same trick works from places like Thailand, Malaysia and China too.

This is a bit like a video game that is supposed to be locked to players in one country, but somehow people abroad still manage to play it. The app is meant to check your location and block you if you are overseas, but this 'location check' does not seem to be working properly.

This discovery raises questions about how well the rule is being enforced, and whether it needs to be fixed quickly. It is a good reminder that even carefully designed rules can have loopholes that clever people find ways around.

lenses.me: Even though you're too young to bet, this shows why rules exist to protect people from spending money they can't afford to lose.
lenses.family: Your parents might talk about why gambling rules matter and how families should manage money carefully.
lenses.friends: You and your classmates might discuss whether it's fair for some people to get around rules using technology.
lenses.nation: Singapore's gambling rules are meant to protect people, so a loophole like this could push authorities to tighten the system.
lenses.world: It shows how tricky it can be for any country to control apps and technology once people travel across borders.

compo_words.meaning: a gap or mistake in a rule that lets people avoid following it
compo_words.example: The clever student found a loophole in the school's homework rules.

compo_words.meaning: to make sure a rule is followed
compo_words.example: The teacher enforced the no-phones rule strictly during exams.
Being vigilant about spending helps you save your pocket money.

compo_phrases.meaning: to fix a gap in a rule so people can no longer break it easily
compo_phrases.example: The company promised to close the loophole in its app.
Parents should keep a tight rein on how much pocket money is spent on games.

questions: Do you think it's fair for people to find clever ways around rules? Why or why not?
Have you ever seen someone break a small rule using a trick? What did you think about it?
Why do you think it's important for grown-ups to be careful with money and betting?

stories.title: Sad Update: Bangkok Bar Fire Death Toll Rises to 34
stories.headline_kid: More than a week after a fire broke out in a Bangkok bar, the number of people who have died has climbed to 34, and many are still fighting for their lives in hospital.
summary: About a week ago, a terrible fire broke out inside a bar in Bangkok, Thailand's capital city. Sadly, the number of people who have died from their injuries keeps rising. On Sunday, a 20-year-old woman passed away in hospital, bringing the total number of deaths to 34.

Doctors say that 13 more people are still in intensive care, which means they are extremely unwell and need constant medical attention just to stay alive. This tragedy has shocked many people, both in Thailand and in nearby countries like Singapore, where many families love to holiday in Bangkok.

Fires in crowded places like bars and clubs are especially dangerous because there are often many people packed into a small space, and it can be hard for everyone to escape quickly if there aren't enough exits. This incident is a painful reminder of why fire safety rules — like having clear exit signs and enough fire extinguishers — are so important.

Investigators are still trying to figure out exactly what caused the fire. Many hope this tragedy will lead to stricter safety checks at nightlife venues across the region, so something like this never happens again.

lenses.me: Whenever you visit a crowded place like a mall or cinema, it's good to notice where the exits are, just in case.
lenses.family: Your family might talk about checking hotel and venue safety when planning holidays abroad.
lenses.friends: You could chat with friends about why fire drills at school are actually really useful.
lenses.nation: Singapore has strict fire safety laws for public venues, and this event is a reminder of why those rules matter so much.
lenses.world: This tragedy may push governments across Southeast Asia to inspect nightlife venues more carefully.

compo_words.meaning: a very sad event, often involving death or great loss
compo_words.example: The fire was a terrible tragedy that shocked the whole city.

compo_words.meaning: the total number of deaths or injuries from an event
compo_words.example: The death toll from the fire continued to rise.

compo_words.meaning: paying close attention to avoid danger
compo_words.example: Staff at the venue should have been more vigilant about fire risks.

compo_phrases.meaning: to be in a very serious medical condition, struggling to survive
compo_phrases.example: Doctors say several patients are still fighting for their lives in hospital.

compo_phrases.meaning: to warn people quickly about danger
compo_phrases.example: A worker raised the alarm as soon as she saw smoke.

questions: Why do you think it's important to know where the fire exits are when you visit a new place?
How would you feel if you heard about a tragedy like this happening somewhere you had visited before?
What can building owners do to keep crowded places safer?

stories.title: How Scammers Hide Behind a Popular Chat App
stories.headline_kid: Investigators in India say scammers love using a chat app called Telegram to cheat people — and Asia, including Singapore, can learn how to fight back.
summary: Telegram is a messaging app a bit like WhatsApp, but it lets people create huge, sometimes secret groups where members don't always know each other. In India, investigators found that scammers have used Telegram again and again — from leaking exam papers before students take them, to tricking people into fake 'get-rich-quick' investment schemes.

Why do scammers love this app so much? Investigators explain that messages on Telegram can be heavily encrypted, which means they're scrambled and very hard for police to read or trace back to a real person. This lets scammers hide their identity while still reaching thousands of victims at once.

This isn't just India's problem. Singapore has also seen scams that start with strangers messaging people on chat apps, promising 'guaranteed' profits or offering suspicious deals. Scam awareness campaigns here often remind people never to trust messages promising money for nothing.

Experts believe Asian countries, including Singapore, can learn from how India is cracking down on this — for example, working closely with tech companies to trace scammers faster and teaching the public how to spot warning signs early.

lenses.me: If a stranger ever messages you about 'easy money' or 'secret answers,' that's usually a big red flag — tell a trusted adult right away.
lenses.family: Your parents can talk with you about never sharing bank details, passwords or OTPs with anyone online, even if they seem friendly.
lenses.friends: Classmates might get added to chat groups promising game cheats or free items — it's good to be cautious about these.
lenses.nation: Singapore regularly sends out anti-scam alerts, and this story shows why staying alert to new scam tricks really matters.
lenses.world: It shows that scam networks often work across many countries, so nations need to cooperate to catch them.

compo_words.meaning: scrambled or coded so it's hard to read without special access
compo_words.example: The scammers used encrypted messages so police couldn't easily trace them.

compo_words.meaning: dishonest and meant to trick people
compo_words.example: The investment scheme turned out to be completely fraudulent.

compo_words.meaning: carefully watching out for danger or trouble
compo_words.example: We must stay vigilant about strange messages from unknown numbers.

compo_phrases.meaning: something that sounds so amazing it's probably a trick
compo_phrases.example: A stranger offering free money online is often too good to be true.

compo_phrases.meaning: to take strong action to stop something bad
compo_phrases.example: Police are cracking down on scam networks using chat apps.

stories.title: Rescued! Survivors Found Days After Boat Sinks Near Indonesia
stories.headline_kid: After a boat sank near Indonesia's Selayar Islands, rescuers found survivors — some floating in the sea, and others stranded on an empty island with no one living there!
summary: A boat carrying passengers sank near the Selayar Islands, off South Sulawesi in Indonesia. In the days that followed, rescue teams worked hard to search the surrounding waters for anyone who might still be alive.

On Saturday, rescuers found five survivors floating in the sea, likely staying afloat using life jackets or pieces of debris from the boat. Then on Sunday, two more survivors were discovered — this time on an uninhabited island, meaning an island where nobody normally lives.

Rescue teams used boats and search planes to cover a wide stretch of ocean, showing just how challenging and important sea rescues can be. Stories like this highlight why safety equipment, such as life jackets, and quick emergency responses can truly save lives.

This is also a gentle reminder for families who love visiting Indonesian islands for holidays — always listen carefully to safety briefings before boarding any boat, no matter how short the trip seems.

lenses.me: It's a good reminder to always wear a life jacket properly if you ever go on a boat trip.
lenses.family: Your family can make it a habit to check safety equipment before any boat or ferry ride during holidays.
lenses.friends: You could talk with friends about what they would do if they were ever stranded and needed to stay calm.
lenses.nation: Singapore, being close to Indonesia, often cooperates on sea rescue and safety efforts in the region.
lenses.world: This story shows how challenging sea rescues can be, and why safety rules for boats matter everywhere.

compo_words.meaning: a place where no people live
compo_words.example: The survivors were found stranded on an uninhabited island.

compo_words.meaning: able to recover quickly from difficulties
compo_words.example: The survivors showed how resilient people can be in a crisis.

compo_words.meaning: left in a place with no way to leave
compo_words.example: They were stranded at sea for days before help arrived.

compo_phrases.example: Against all odds, the survivors were found alive after days at sea.

stories.title: Spain Wins the World Cup After a Dramatic Final!
stories.headline_kid: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 in extra time to become World Cup champions, in a final packed with drama, a red card, and a booing crowd!
summary: Spain has won the World Cup, beating Argentina 1-0 after the match went into extra time. This means the game finished level after the normal 90 minutes, so both teams had to play an extra 30 minutes to decide the winner.

The final got even more dramatic when Argentina had a player sent off with a red card, leaving them to finish the match with only 10 players instead of 11 — a huge disadvantage in football. After the final whistle, tempers flared briefly between players, though things soon calmed down.

During the trophy ceremony, the crowd loudly booed as US President Donald Trump and FIFA president Gianni Infantino walked onto the field. Big sporting events like the World Cup often draw strong reactions from fans, especially when political figures are involved.

Even in Singapore, football fans stayed up late or gathered at coffee shops and homes to watch the final together, cheering, gasping, and celebrating with friends and family — proof that football truly brings people together, no matter where they live.

lenses.me: Think about your own favourite football moments — did you stay up to watch this final too?
lenses.family: Many families in Singapore probably watched the match together late at night, sharing snacks and excitement.
lenses.friends: You and your classmates are probably still talking about the match and the dramatic ending at school.
lenses.nation: Football is hugely popular in Singapore, and big matches like this often unite fans across the country.
lenses.world: The World Cup happens once every four years and brings together countries from around the globe in friendly competition.

compo_words.example: The dramatic final kept fans on the edge of their seats.

compo_words.meaning: a condition that puts someone at a weaker position
compo_words.example: Playing with 10 men put Argentina at a huge disadvantage.

compo_words.meaning: feeling or showing great joy at a victory
compo_words.example: The Spanish players were triumphant after lifting the trophy.

compo_phrases.meaning: people suddenly became angry or upset
compo_phrases.example: Tempers flared between players after the final whistle.
Spain fought hard against the odds after Argentina had 11 players.
